reason: Refutation fails on every angle; the change is well-grounded. (1) Verified the recall 0.50 is a FIXTURE-grading bug, not a prompt/threshold defect: all 3 baseline repeats of bad/client-pact-divergence (findings/contracts/bad/client-pact-divergence__r0..r2.json) returned exactly one *info* finding (stale pact + the optional `data.get('location')` read). The change.patch never introduces a new HARD dependency (the `int(data['in_stock'])` read is unchanged), so 'info' is the CORRECT reviewer verdict; graded against block_threshold=high it simply registers no blocking finding. The sibling bad/client-missing-pact-field scores high 3/3, so aggregate bad-recall = 1/2 = 0.50 exactly as diagnosed. (2) No under-flagging is introduced: the proposal KEEPS the prompt's three high categories verbatim (provider break / uncompensated incompatible response change / weakened consumer assertion) and only PREPENDS the rubric plus a hard-vs-optional clarifier that codifies the reviewers' already-correct behavior (hard `data['field']`->KeyError = high; optional .get()/stale pact = info). No high category is removed or loosened. (3) No threshold is loosened: block_threshold stays 'high' (unchanged); the fix makes the bad fixture genuinely high rather than lowering the bar to admit the info-tier case. (4) The good fixture is verifiably clean: client-pact-covered scores [] 3/3 (fp 0.00), so needs_second_good_fixture=false is justified and the prompt was NOT tuned against a dirty good fixture. (5) The fixture edits sharpen rather than blur: redesigning client-pact-divergence's optional `.get()` read into a HARD undeclared `int(data['field'])` dependency makes the bad case LESS ambiguous and maps cleanly onto the prompt's existing high category, and the hard undeclared pact read is squarely contracts' domain (rubric assigns contract/spec parity to contracts), not a scope violation; the added weakened-consumer-assertion fixture covers a real but previously-untested high category. No concrete defect-class under-flag, no known-bad slip-through, no dirty good fixture, no ambiguous bad case -> nothing to refute.
